variants.doc: CR 702: Matches objects that do NOT have a given keyword ability. Used for "without flying", "without first strike", etc.

variants.name: HasAnyCounter
variants.doc: CR 122.1: Matches objects with at least one counter of any type on them. Used for "creature with one or more counters on it" phrases where the counter type is unspecified (Nils, Discipline Enforcer's attack-tax class).

variants.name: CmcGE
variants.doc: Matches objects with converted mana cost >= N (for "mana value N or greater"). CR 202.3: Uses QuantityExpr to support both fixed and dynamic comparisons.

variants.name: CmcLE
variants.doc: Matches objects with converted mana cost <= N (for "mana value N or less"). CR 202.3: Uses QuantityExpr to support both fixed and dynamic comparisons.

variants.name: AttachedToSource
variants.doc: CR 301.5 + CR 303.4: True when the matched object's `attached_to` field equals the filter source's object ID. Inverse of `EnchantedBy`/`EquippedBy`, which check whether the source has an attachment. Used for "Aura and Equipment attached to ~" quantity clauses (Kellan, the Fae-Blooded) and for any compound filter whose subject is "attached to <self>".

variants.name: AttachedToRecipient
variants.doc: CR 301.5 + CR 303.4 + CR 613.4c: True when the matched object's `attached_to` field equals the *recipient* of the resolving effect (the per-object `id` in a layer's affected list). Distinct from `AttachedToSource` — for an Aura/Equipment static "Enchanted/Equipped creature gets +N/+M for each X attached to it", the pronoun "it" refers to the affected creature, not to the static's source object. The recipient is supplied through `FilterContext::recipient_id`; when recipient is unknown (no per-recipient context), this prop evaluates to false. Covers ~25 cards: Strong Back, Mantle of the Ancients, Auramancer's Guise, Champion of the Flame, Bruenor Battlehammer's "Each creature you control gets +2/+0 for each Equipment attached to it", and the broader "<subject> gets +N/+M for each Aura/Equipment attached to it" family.

variants.name: HasAttachment
variants.doc: CR 303.4 + CR 301.5: Matches objects that have at least one attachment of the given kind whose controller matches `controller`. Unlike `EnchantedBy`/`EquippedBy` (which are source-relative — match when THIS source is attached to the object), this predicate is non-source-relative: it matches any object with a qualifying attachment. `controller = None` means "any controller".  Covers: - "enchanted creature" when the ability source is not the Aura itself   (e.g. Hateful Eidolon's "Whenever an enchanted creature dies, ..."). - "creature enchanted by an Aura you control" (Killian, Decisive Mentor). - "creature equipped by an Equipment you control" (future).

variants.name: HasAnyAttachmentOf
variants.doc: CR 303.4 + CR 301.5: Disjunctive attachment predicate — matches objects that have at least one attachment whose subtype is in `kinds` and whose controller satisfies the optional `ControllerRef`. Generalizes `HasAttachment` to the "enchanted or equipped" compound subject class (Reyav, Master Smith; Dogmeat, Ever Loyal). Single-kind use is also valid (kinds.len() == 1) but `HasAttachment` is preferred for that case.

variants.name: Another
variants.doc: Matches any object that is NOT the trigger source (for "another creature" triggers).

variants.name: OtherThanTriggerObject
variants.doc: CR 603.4 + CR 109.3: Matches any object that is NOT the object that caused the currently-evaluating trigger to fire (the "triggering object"). Distinct from `Another`, which excludes the *ability source*. Used for intervening-if count predicates like Valakut, the Molten Pinnacle's "if you control at least five other Mountains" — here "other" means "other than the newly- entered Mountain," not "other than Valakut." Resolves against `FilterContext::triggering_object_id`, populated at trigger-condition evaluation from the current `GameEvent`.

variants.name: HasColor
variants.doc: Matches objects with a specific color (for "white creature", "red spell", etc.).

variants.name: PowerLE
variants.doc: Matches objects with power <= N (for "creature with power 2 or less"). CR 208.1: Uses QuantityExpr to support both fixed and dynamic comparisons.

variants.name: PowerGE
variants.doc: Matches objects with power >= N (for "creature with power 3 or greater"). CR 208.1: Uses QuantityExpr to support both fixed and dynamic comparisons.

variants.name: PowerGTSource
variants.doc: CR 509.1b: Matches objects whose power is strictly greater than the source object's power. Used for "creatures with greater power" blocking restrictions (relative comparison).

variants.name: Multicolored
variants.doc: Matches multicolored objects (2+ colors).

variants.name: Colorless
variants.doc: CR 105.2c: Matches colorless objects (0 colors).

variants.name: HasSupertype
variants.doc: Matches objects with a specific supertype (Basic, Legendary, Snow).

variants.name: IsChosenCreatureType
variants.doc: Matches objects whose subtypes include the source object's chosen creature type. Used for "of the chosen type" patterns (Cavern of Souls, Metallic Mimic).

variants.name: IsChosenColor
variants.doc: Matches objects whose colors include the source object's chosen color. Used for "of the chosen color" patterns (Hall of Triumph, Runed Stalactite). Reads `ChosenAttribute::Color` from the source permanent.

variants.name: IsChosenCardType
variants.doc: Matches objects whose core type includes the source object's chosen card type. Used for "spells of the chosen type" patterns (Archon of Valor's Reach). Reads `ChosenAttribute::CardType` from the source permanent.

variants.name: HasSingleTarget
variants.doc: CR 115.7: Matches stack entries that have exactly one target. Used for "with a single target" qualifiers on retarget effects.

variants.name: NotColor
variants.doc: CR 205.4b: Matches objects that do NOT have a specific color. Parallel to `HasColor` — used for "nonblack", "nonwhite" in negation stacks.

variants.name: NotSupertype
variants.doc: CR 205.4a: Matches objects that do NOT have a specific supertype. Parallel to `HasSupertype` — used for "nonbasic", "nonlegendary" in negation stacks.

variants.name: Suspected
variants.doc: CR 701.60b: Matches suspected creatures.

variants.name: ToughnessGTPower
variants.doc: CR 510.1c: Matches creatures whose toughness is greater than their power.

variants.name: ToughnessLE
variants.doc: CR 208.1: Matches objects with toughness <= N. Mirrors `PowerLE`. Used for "creature with toughness N or less" and as a building block for disjunctive P/T filters ("power or toughness N or less").

variants.name: ToughnessGE
variants.doc: CR 208.1: Matches objects with toughness >= N. Mirrors `PowerGE`.

variants.name: AnyOf
variants.doc: Disjunctive composite: the object matches if ANY inner prop matches. Used for natural-language OR within a property suffix — e.g. "creature with power or toughness N or less" decomposes to `AnyOf { [PowerLE(N), ToughnessLE(N)] }` on a `creature` typed filter, preserving the single-type constraint while expressing the OR semantics at the property layer. Nest by composing with other props.

variants.name: Modified
variants.doc: CR 700.9: A permanent is modified if it has one or more counters on it (CR 122), if it is equipped (CR 301.5), or if it is enchanted by an Aura that is controlled by that permanent's controller (CR 303.4).  Modeled as a first-class typed predicate rather than an `AnyOf` composite because CR 700.9 names "modified" as a distinct concept and the three legs share a single runtime match arm. Parser dispatch emits `FilterProp::Modified` for "modified creature(s)" subjects, analogous to how `FilterProp::Suspected` models CR 701.60b's "suspected" status.

variants.name: Historic
variants.doc: CR 700.6: An object is historic if it has the legendary supertype, the artifact card type, or the Saga subtype.  Modeled as a first-class typed predicate rather than an `AnyOf` composite because CR 700.6 names "historic" as a distinct concept and the three legs share a single runtime match arm. Parser dispatch emits `FilterProp::Historic` for "historic permanent" / "historic spell" / "historic card" subjects, mirroring `FilterProp::Modified` for CR 700.9's "modified" predicate.

variants.name: DifferentNameFrom
variants.doc: Matches objects whose name differs from all objects matching the inner filter that the evaluating controller controls on the battlefield. Used for "with a different name than each [type] you control" (e.g. Light-Paws).

variants.name: InAnyZone
variants.doc: CR 604.3: Matches objects whose current zone is any of the listed zones (OR semantics). Used for zone-based restrictions like "cards in graveyards and libraries".

variants.name: SharesQuality
variants.doc: CR 700.5: Multi-target group constraint — all selected targets must share at least one value of the named quality. Validated at resolution time, not per-object. Examples: "that share a creature type", "that share a color", "that share a card type".

variants.name: WasDealtDamageThisTurn
variants.doc: CR 510.1: Object was dealt damage during this turn. Checks `damage_marked > 0` (damage persists until cleanup step).

variants.name: EnteredThisTurn
variants.doc: CR 400.7: Object entered the battlefield during this turn. Checks `entered_battlefield_turn == Some(current_turn)`.

variants.name: AttackedThisTurn
variants.doc: CR 508.1a: Creature was declared as an attacker this turn. Checks `creatures_attacked_this_turn` tracking set on GameState.

variants.name: BlockedThisTurn
variants.doc: CR 509.1a: Creature was declared as a blocker this turn. Checks `creatures_blocked_this_turn` tracking set on GameState.

variants.name: AttackedOrBlockedThisTurn
variants.doc: CR 508.1a + CR 509.1a: Creature attacked or blocked this turn. Compound check used by "that attacked or blocked this turn" Oracle text.

variants.name: FaceDown
variants.doc: CR 707.2: Matches face-down objects on the battlefield. Used for "face-down creature" trigger subjects.

variants.name: TargetsOnly
variants.doc: CR 115.9c: Matches stack entries whose targets ALL satisfy the given filter. Used for "that targets only ~", "that targets only a single creature you control", etc. Permissive at the per-object filter level; validated against the stack entry's actual targets by trigger matchers and retarget effects.

variants.name: Targets
variants.doc: CR 115.9b: Matches stack entries that have at least one target satisfying the filter. Used for "that targets ~", "that targets you", etc. (.any() semantics). Contrast with TargetsOnly (CR 115.9c) which requires ALL targets to match (.all()).

variants.name: CmcEQ
variants.doc: Matches objects with converted mana cost == N (for "with mana value N" exact match). CR 202.3: Uses QuantityExpr to support both fixed and dynamic comparisons.

variants.name: HasXInManaCost
variants.doc: CR 107.3 + CR 202.1: Matches spells/objects whose printed mana cost contains an `{X}` shard. Used for "spell with {X} in its mana cost" qualifier on spell-cast triggers (Lattice Library, Nev the Practical Dean, Owlin Spiralmancer, Brass Infiniscope, Elementalist's Palette). Evaluated against `SpellCastRecord.has_x_in_cost` in the spell-history filter path and against `cost_has_x(&obj.mana_cost)` for live objects.

variants.name: Named
variants.doc: CR 201.2: Matches objects whose card name equals the given name. Used for "cards named [X]" and "named [X]" filter patterns. Name comparison is exact per CR 201.2a (case-insensitive at evaluation).

variants.name: SameName
variants.doc: Matches objects with the same name as a previously-referenced card. Used for "search your library for a card with that name" patterns.

variants.name: SameNameAsParentTarget
variants.doc: CR 201.2: Matches objects whose name equals the name of the resolving ability's first object target. Used by chained sub-abilities where a prior step targeted/exiled a card and the next step references "cards with that name" — e.g., Deadly Cover-Up's "search ... for any number of cards with that name and exile them" (the "that name" is the name of the card exiled by the immediately preceding effect, which is inherited as the first target via `TargetFilter::ParentTarget`).  Differs from `SameName` (which reads the source object's name): this reads from `ability.targets[0]` when that target is `TargetRef::Object`, looking up the name from `state.objects` (or `lki_cache` if the target has already left its zone).

variants.name: NameMatchesAnyPermanent
variants.doc: CR 201.2 + CR 201.2a: Matches objects whose name equals the name of any permanent currently on the battlefield. `controller` optionally narrows the pool of permanents whose names are considered (None = any controller, i.e. "shares a name with a permanent" unqualified). Used by "put a card onto the battlefield if it has the same name as a permanent" patterns (Mitotic Manipulation, and any analogous dig-with-name-match effect).

variants.name: AttackingController
variants.doc: CR 508.1b: Matches attacking creatures whose defending player equals the filter's source controller ("creatures attacking you"). Distinct from `Attacking`, which matches any attacker regardless of defender.

variants.name: IsCommander
variants.doc: CR 903.3 + CR 903.3d: Matches permanents on the battlefield that are a commander. Reads `GameObject::is_commander`, set during deck construction per CR 903.3 (the legendary card designated as that deck's commander). Used for "commander(s) you control", "your commander" subject phrases and for "target commander" in commander-format effects (Codsworth, Falthis, Anara, Champions of Archery, etc.).

FlashbackCost.doc: CR 702.34a: Flashback cost — either a mana cost or a non-mana cost (e.g., "Tap three untapped white creatures you control").

FormatGroup.doc: Broad grouping used by the UI to visually cluster related formats (constructed, commander-style, multiplayer). Frontends may key color treatments off the group so they don't have to maintain a per-format styling table.

GainLifePlayer.doc: Who gains life from a GainLife effect.

variants.name: Controller
variants.doc: The ability's controller (default).

variants.name: TargetedController
variants.doc: The controller of the targeted permanent.

variants.name: UntapLandForMana
variants.doc: CR 605.3a: Undo a manual mana ability activation — untap source, remove produced mana. Only valid for lands in `lands_tapped_for_mana` whose mana hasn't been spent.

variants.name: CrewVehicle
variants.doc: CR 702.122a: Crew a Vehicle by tapping creatures with total power >= N. During Priority: creature_ids is empty (triggers state transition). During CrewVehicle: creature_ids contains the selected creatures.

variants.name: ActivateStation
variants.doc: CR 702.184a: Activate a Spacecraft's station ability. During Priority: creature_id is None (triggers state transition to `WaitingFor::StationTarget`). During StationTarget: creature_id is `Some(id)` — the single creature being tapped to station.

variants.name: SaddleMount
variants.doc: CR 702.171a: Saddle a Mount by tapping creatures with total power >= N. During Priority: creature_ids is empty (triggers state transition to `WaitingFor::SaddleMount`). During SaddleMount: creature_ids contains the selected creatures.

variants.name: ChooseAdventureFace
variants.doc: CR 715.3a: Choose creature face (true) or Adventure half (false).

variants.name: ChooseModalFace
variants.doc: CR 712.12: Choose front face (false) or back face (true) for MDFC land play.

variants.name: ChooseWarpCost
variants.doc: Choose normal cast (false) or Warp cast (true) from hand.

variants.name: ChooseEvokeCost
variants.doc: CR 702.74a: Choose normal cast (false) or Evoke cast (true) from hand. Evoke cast tags the resolving permanent so the synthesized intervening-if ETB sacrifice trigger fires.

variants.name: ChooseOverloadCost
variants.doc: CR 702.96a: Choose normal cast (false) or Overload cast (true) from hand. Overload cast substitutes the overload mana cost and transforms every "target" in the spell's text to "each" (CR 702.96b-c).

variants.name: ActivateNinjutsu
variants.doc: CR 702.49: Activate a Ninjutsu-family keyword from hand or command zone during combat.

variants.name: CastSpellAsSneak
variants.doc: CR 702.190a: Cast a spell from HAND via the Sneak alternative cost. Legal only during the declare-blockers step (CR 702.190a). Applies to any card type (creature, artifact, sorcery, instant, …) — the printed keyword's cost grants permission regardless of the card's core type.  `creature_to_return` must be an unblocked attacker controlled by the casting player; it is returned to its owner's hand as part of paying the Sneak cost (CR 702.190a).  CR 702.190b applies only to permanent spells: they enter tapped and attacking alongside the returned creature. Non-permanent Sneak casts resolve normally.

variants.name: CastSpellForFree
variants.doc: CR 601.2b + CR 118.9a: Cast a spell from hand for free via a `StaticMode::CastFromHandFree` permission source (Zaffai and the Tempests — "Once during each of your turns, you may cast an instant or sorcery spell from your hand without paying its mana cost").  The implicit Omniscience silent-free path uses `GameAction::CastSpell` with `CastingVariant::Normal` and a `NoCost` short-circuit — this dedicated action variant is reserved for `OncePerTurn` permissions where the player's "may cast" choice and the source-slot consumption must be visible at the action layer.

variants.name: CastSpellAsMiracle
variants.doc: CR 702.94a + CR 603.11: Accept a pending `WaitingFor::MiracleReveal` and cast `object_id` from hand for the card's miracle mana cost. Mirror of `CastSpellAsSneak` / `CastSpellForFree` — dedicated variant because the cast is opted into from a specialized prompt, not from Priority. Decline is via the shared `DecideOptionalEffect { accept: false }`.

variants.name: CastSpellAsMadness
variants.doc: CR 702.35a: Accept a pending `WaitingFor::MadnessCastOffer` and cast `object_id` from exile for its madness cost. Decline is via the shared `DecideOptionalEffect { accept: false }`.

variants.name: DecideOptionalEffect
variants.doc: CR 609.3: Accept or decline an optional effect ("You may X").

variants.name: PayUnlessCost
variants.doc: CR 118.12: Pay or decline an "unless pays" cost (e.g., Mana Leak, No More Lies).

variants.name: PayCombatTax
variants.doc: CR 508.1d + CR 508.1h + CR 509.1c + CR 509.1d: Pay or decline the aggregate combat tax (Ghostly Prison, Propaganda, Sphere of Safety, Windborn Muse). On accept the engine deducts the locked-in total and completes the paused attack/block declaration; on decline the engine strips the taxed creatures from the declaration and completes with the remaining, untaxed subset.

variants.name: ChooseRingBearer
variants.doc: CR 701.54a: Choose a creature to be the ring-bearer.

variants.name: ChooseDungeon
variants.doc: CR 701.49a: Choose which dungeon to venture into.

variants.name: ChooseDungeonRoom
variants.doc: CR 309.5a: Choose which room to advance to at a branch point.

variants.name: TapForConvoke
variants.doc: CR 702.51a: Tap creature/artifact for convoke or waterbend mana. CR 302.6: Summoning sickness does not apply (convoke doesn't use the tap ability mechanism).

variants.name: HarmonizeTap
variants.doc: CR 702.180a/b: Harmonize — optionally tap a creature to reduce casting cost by its power. None = skip (decline the cost reduction).

variants.name: DeclareCompanion
variants.doc: CR 702.139a: Declare a companion during pre-game reveal (or decline).

variants.name: CompanionToHand
variants.doc: CR 702.139a: Pay {3} to put companion into hand (special action, see rule 116.2g).

variants.name: DiscoverChoice
variants.doc: CR 701.57a: Choose to cast discovered card or put it to hand.

variants.name: CascadeChoice
variants.doc: CR 702.85a: Choose to cast the cascaded card without paying its mana cost.

variants.name: ChooseTopOrBottom
variants.doc: CR 401.4: Choose top or bottom of library.

variants.name: ChooseLegend
variants.doc: CR 704.5j: Choose which legendary permanent to keep.

variants.name: ChooseBattleProtector
variants.doc: CR 310.10 + CR 704.5w + CR 704.5x: Choose which player becomes the battle's new protector when the SBA pauses with a `BattleProtectorChoice`.

variants.name: SetAutoPass
variants.doc: Set auto-pass mode for the acting player (CR 117.4).

variants.name: CancelAutoPass
variants.doc: Cancel any active auto-pass for the acting player.

variants.name: SetPhaseStops
variants.doc: Replace the acting player's phase-stop preference list. Phase stops interrupt an `UntilEndOfTurn` auto-pass session and prevent the engine from auto-submitting empty blocker declarations during the named phases. Legal in any WaitingFor state — pure preference propagation.

variants.name: AssignCombatDamage
variants.doc: CR 510.1c/d: Assign damage from an attacker to its blockers (and optionally the defending player/PW with trample, plus PW controller with trample-over-PW).

variants.name: DistributeAmong
variants.doc: CR 601.2d: Distribute N among targets at casting time.

variants.name: SubmitPayAmount
variants.doc: CR 107.1c + CR 107.14: Submit the chosen amount for a `WaitingFor::PayAmountChoice` prompt ("pay any amount of {E}" and similar resource-choice patterns).

variants.name: RetargetSpell
variants.doc: CR 115.7: Choose new target(s) for a spell or ability on the stack.

variants.name: LearnDecision
variants.doc: CR 701.48a: Learn — choose to rummage (discard a card, draw a card) or skip.

variants.name: SelectCategoryPermanents
variants.doc: CR 101.4 + CR 701.21a: Select one permanent per type category to keep; the rest will be sacrificed. Each position corresponds to a category in `WaitingFor::CategoryChoice::categories`. `None` = no permanent of that type.

variants.name: ChooseX
variants.doc: CR 107.1b + CR 601.2f: Choose the value of X for a spell or activated ability whose cost contains X. Chosen as part of determining total cost, before mana is paid.

variants.name: SubmitPhyrexianChoices
variants.doc: CR 107.4f + CR 601.2f: Caster submits their per-shard payment choice (mana or 2 life) for each Phyrexian shard in the spell's cost. The length of `choices` MUST equal `WaitingFor::PhyrexianPayment.shards.len()`.

variants.name: ChooseManaColor
variants.doc: CR 605.3b: Answer the `WaitingFor::ChooseManaColor` prompt. Shape mirrors the prompt variant (`SingleColor` or `Combination`).

variants.name: PayManaAbilityMana
variants.doc: CR 605.3a + CR 601.2h + CR 107.4e: Answer the `WaitingFor::PayManaAbilityMana` prompt by picking one of the legal per-hybrid-shard color vectors. `payment.len()` equals the number of hybrid shards in the ability's `Mana` sub-cost. The engine verifies the vector is present in the prompt's `options` before debiting.

variants.name: CastPreparedCopy
variants.doc: CR 702.xxx: Prepare (Strixhaven) — at priority, cast a token copy of a prepared creature's face-`b` prepare-spell. The source creature must have `prepared.is_some()` and be controlled by the acting player. On cast, the source becomes unprepared (single-authority via `effects::prepare::unprepare_object`). Assign when WotC publishes SOS CR update.

variants.name: CastParadigmCopy
variants.doc: CR 702.xxx: Paradigm (Strixhaven) — accept the turn-based offer during `WaitingFor::ParadigmCastOffer`, casting a token copy of the exiled source spell without paying its mana cost. The exiled source stays in exile. Assign when WotC publishes SOS CR update.

variants.name: PassParadigmOffer
variants.doc: CR 702.xxx: Paradigm (Strixhaven) — decline the turn-based offer during `WaitingFor::ParadigmCastOffer`. The exiled source stays in exile and may be offered again next turn. Assign when WotC publishes SOS CR update.

variants.name: Concede
variants.doc: CR 104.3a: A player may concede the game at any time. That player leaves the game. CR 800.4a: When a player leaves a multiplayer game, all objects owned by that player leave the game and all spells/abilities controlled by that player cease to exist.  Concede is always legal regardless of priority or `WaitingFor` state — the action handler bypasses the normal `(WaitingFor, GameAction)` match dispatch and delegates directly to `eliminate_player`. It is intentionally NOT included in `legal_actions()` enumeration; callers (UI, network layer) surface it directly.

variants.name: XValueChosen
variants.doc: CR 107.1b + CR 601.2f: The caster has chosen the value of X for a pending cast whose cost contained `ManaCostShard::X`.

variants.name: ZoneChanged
variants.doc: CR 603.6a: Enters-the-battlefield and zone-change triggers fire on this event. `from` is `None` when an object is created directly in a zone without a prior zone — e.g., a token is created on the battlefield (CR 111.1 + CR 603.6a: "an object that enters the battlefield as a token is created in the battlefield zone"). Treating token creation as a `ZoneChanged` event means every ETB trigger matcher (Elvish Vanguard, Soul Warden, Panharmonicon) automatically fires for tokens without bespoke per-matcher code paths.

variants.name: PermanentPhasedOut
variants.doc: CR 702.26b: A permanent phased out (status changed to phased out). `indirect` is true iff this permanent was phased out because a host it was attached to phased out (CR 702.26g).

variants.name: PermanentPhasedIn
variants.doc: CR 702.26c: A permanent phased in (status changed to phased in).

variants.name: PlayerPhasedOut
variants.doc: A player phased out. Player phasing is not formally governed by CR 702.26 (which is permanent-only); semantics mirror the permanent rule and are driven by the small set of card Oracle text that says "you phase out". While phased out, the player is excluded from targeting, attacking, damage, and the 0-or-less life SBA.

variants.name: PlayerPhasedIn
variants.doc: A player phased back in (typically at the start of their next turn or when an `UntilYourNextTurn` duration ends).

variants.name: DamagePrevented
variants.doc: CR 615: Damage was prevented (by a prevention shield or protection). Enables "when damage is prevented" triggers.

variants.name: ObjectConjured
variants.doc: Digital-only: A card was conjured from outside the game into a zone.

variants.name: CombatTaxPaid
variants.doc: CR 508.1h + CR 509.1d: The aggregate combat tax was paid; the declaration proceeds with every declared creature intact.

variants.name: CombatTaxDeclined
variants.doc: CR 508.1d + CR 509.1c: The combat tax was declined; the listed taxed creatures have been dropped from the declaration before it completes.

variants.name: VehicleCrewed
variants.doc: CR 702.122d: A Vehicle's crew ability resolved. Carries creature list for trigger conditions that reference "creatures that crewed it".

variants.name: Stationed
variants.doc: CR 702.184a: A Spacecraft's station ability resolved. Fires the `TriggerMode::Stationed` event for triggers on the Spacecraft that care about the act of being stationed. Carries the tapped creature and the number of counters added so downstream consumers (logs, future "whenever ~ is stationed by a creature with X" triggers) can see the inputs without re-deriving them.

variants.name: Saddled
variants.doc: CR 702.171a: A Mount's saddle ability resolved. Fires the `TriggerMode::Saddled` / `TriggerMode::BecomesSaddled` events for triggers that care about the act of being saddled. Carries the tapped creatures so trigger conditions referencing "creatures that saddled it" can resolve against last-known information.

variants.name: Regenerated
variants.doc: CR 701.19a: Regeneration shield — consumed on use, expires at cleanup.

variants.name: CreatureSuspected
variants.doc: CR 701.60a: A creature was suspected.

variants.name: BecamePrepared
variants.doc: CR 702.xxx: Prepare (Strixhaven) — a creature became prepared. Emitted only when the toggle actually flips (idempotent resolvers). Assign when WotC publishes SOS CR update.

variants.name: BecameUnprepared
variants.doc: CR 702.xxx: Prepare (Strixhaven) — a creature became unprepared. Emitted only when the toggle actually flips (idempotent resolvers). Assign when WotC publishes SOS CR update.

variants.name: CaseSolved
variants.doc: CR 719.3b: A Case enchantment became solved.

variants.name: ClassLevelGained
variants.doc: CR 716.2a: A Class enchantment gained a new level.

variants.name: MonarchChanged
variants.doc: CR 725: A player became the monarch.

variants.name: CityBlessingGained
variants.doc: CR 702.131b: A player gained the city's blessing (Ascend).

variants.name: DieRolled
variants.doc: CR 706: A die was rolled.

variants.name: CoinFlipped
variants.doc: CR 705: A coin was flipped.

variants.name: RingTemptsYou
variants.doc: CR 701.54: The Ring tempted a player.

variants.name: RoomEntered
variants.doc: CR 309.4c: A player moved their venture marker into a dungeon room.

variants.name: DungeonCompleted
variants.doc: CR 309.7: A player completed a dungeon (removed from game).

variants.name: InitiativeTaken
variants.doc: CR 725: A player took the initiative.

variants.name: Firebend
variants.doc: Avatar crossover: A creature with firebending attacked, producing mana.

variants.name: Airbend
variants.doc: Avatar crossover: A permanent or spell was airbent (exiled with alt-cast permission).

variants.name: Earthbend
variants.doc: Avatar crossover: A land was earthbent (animated with counters + return trigger).

variants.name: Waterbend
variants.doc: Avatar crossover: A waterbend cost was paid (tap-to-pay for generic mana).

variants.name: CompanionRevealed
variants.doc: CR 702.139a: Companion revealed at game start.

variants.name: CompanionMovedToHand
variants.doc: CR 702.139a: Companion moved to hand via {3} special action.

variants.name: NinjutsuActivated
variants.doc: CR 702.49a: A ninjutsu-family ability was activated (ninjutsu, commander ninjutsu, sneak). This is a special action, not an activated ability on the stack, so it does not fire AbilityActivated. Enables "whenever you activate a ninjutsu ability" triggers.

variants.name: CreatureExploited
variants.doc: CR 702.110: A creature exploited another creature (sacrificed via exploit ETB).

variants.name: EnergyChanged
variants.doc: CR 122.1: A player's energy counter total changed.

variants.name: SpeedChanged
variants.doc: CR 702.179: A player's speed changed.

variants.name: PlayerCounterChanged
variants.doc: CR 122.1: A player counter (poison, experience, rad, ticket, etc.) changed.

variants.name: ManaExpended
variants.doc: CR 700.14: Mana was spent on a spell cast, updating the cumulative total this turn.

variants.name: Clash
variants.doc: CR 701.30: A clash occurred between two players.

variants.name: VoteCast
variants.doc: CR 701.38a: A player cast a single vote in a Council's-dilemma resolution. One event per vote (so a player with multiple votes produces multiple events). `choice` is the lowercase canonical option name from `Effect::Vote.choices`.

variants.name: VoteResolved
variants.doc: CR 701.38: All voters have voted. Emitted before the per-choice tally sub-effects fire. `tallies` is `(choice, count)` pairs in `options` declaration order.

variants.name: PowerToughnessChanged
variants.doc: Emitted when layer re-evaluation changes a creature's effective power/toughness. Generic event — not tied to any specific card or effect.

variants.name: CascadeMissed
variants.doc: CR 702.85a: Cascade exiled the entire library (or whatever remained after replacement effects) without finding a nonland card with `mana_value < source_mv`. Emitted before the bottom-shuffle so the log/UI can announce the miss without inferring it from absence.

GameRestriction.doc: A game-level restriction that modifies how rules are applied. Stored in `GameState::restrictions` and evaluated by relevant game systems.

variants.name: DamagePreventionDisabled
variants.doc: CR 614.16: Damage prevention effects are suppressed.

variants.name: CastOnlyFromZones
variants.doc: CR 101.2 + CR 601.2a: A temporary effect restricts affected players to casting spells only from the listed zones until the restriction expires.

variants.name: CantCastSpells
variants.doc: CR 101.2: A temporary effect prevents affected players from casting any spell until the restriction expires. E.g., Silence: "Your opponents can't cast spells this turn."

GiftKind.doc: The type of gift promised by the Gift keyword.

variants.name: Card
variants.doc: Opponent draws a card.

variants.name: Treasure
variants.doc: Opponent creates a Treasure token.

variants.name: Food
variants.doc: Opponent creates a Food token.

variants.name: TappedFish
variants.doc: Opponent creates a tapped 1/1 blue Fish creature token.

HandSizeModification.doc: CR 402.2: How a static ability modifies the maximum hand size.

variants.name: SetTo
variants.doc: "Your maximum hand size is N." — overrides the base hand size.

variants.name: AdjustedBy
variants.doc: "Your maximum hand size is increased/reduced by N." — adjusts the base hand size.

variants.name: EqualTo
variants.doc: "Your maximum hand size is equal to [quantity]." — dynamic quantity from game state.

HexproofFilter.doc: CR 702.11d: What a hexproof-from keyword protects against. Mirrors ProtectionTarget but only applies to targeting (not DEBT).

variants.name: CardType
variants.doc: "hexproof from artifacts", "hexproof from instants", "hexproof from planeswalkers"

variants.name: Quality
variants.doc: "hexproof from monocolored", "hexproof from multicolored"

Keyword.doc: All MTG keywords as typed enum variants. Simple (unit) variants for keywords with no parameters. Parameterized variants carry associated data (ManaCost for costs, amounts, etc.). Unknown captures any unrecognized keyword string for forward compatibility.  Custom Deserialize: accepts both the typed externally-tagged format (new) and plain "Name:Param" strings (legacy card-data.json).

variants.name: TrampleOverPlaneswalkers
variants.doc: CR 702.19c: Trample over planeswalkers — excess damage can spill to PW controller.

variants.name: HexproofFrom
variants.doc: CR 702.11d: "Hexproof from [quality]" — prevents targeting by sources with the quality.

variants.name: Afflict
variants.doc: CR 702.130a: "Afflict N" — when blocked, defending player loses N life.

variants.name: StartYourEngines
variants.doc: CR 702.179: Grants the player a speed value via SBA and enables the inherent speed trigger.

variants.name: Waterbend
variants.doc: Waterbend: tap-to-pay keyword for Avatar waterbending abilities.

variants.name: JobSelect
variants.doc: CR 702.182a: Job select — "When this Equipment enters, create a 1/1 colorless Hero creature token, then attach this Equipment to it."

variants.name: Partner
variants.doc: CR 702.124: Partner and its variant keywords for co-commander pairing.

variants.name: Companion
variants.doc: CR 702.139: Companion — deckbuilding restriction that allows this card to be declared as a companion from outside the game.

variants.name: CommanderNinjutsu
variants.doc: CR 702.49d: Commander ninjutsu — activate from hand or command zone.

variants.name: Miracle
variants.doc: CR 702.94a: Miracle {cost} — static ability linked (CR 603.11) to a triggered ability. Static: "You may reveal this card from your hand as you draw it if it's the first card you've drawn this turn." Linked trigger: "When you reveal this card this way, you may cast it by paying [cost] rather than its mana cost." Runtime support: draw event populates `first_card_drawn_this_turn`; on that event a `WaitingFor::MiracleReveal` prompt is offered for miracle-keyworded cards. Casting uses `CastingVariant::Miracle` with the miracle mana cost.

variants.name: Escape
variants.doc: CR 702.138: Escape — cast from graveyard for an alternative cost, exiling N other cards from your graveyard as an additional cost.

variants.name: Harmonize
variants.doc: CR 702.180: Harmonize {cost} — cast from graveyard for harmonize cost, tap up to one creature to reduce cost by its power, exile on resolution.

variants.name: Buyback
variants.doc: CR 702.27a: Buyback — optional additional cost; if paid, the spell returns to its owner's hand instead of the graveyard as it resolves.

variants.name: Casualty
variants.doc: CR 702.153a: Casualty N — as an additional cost, you may sacrifice a creature with power N or greater. When you do, copy this spell.

variants.name: Entwine
variants.doc: CR 702.42a: Entwine — pay additional cost to choose all modes of a modal spell.

variants.name: LevelUp
variants.doc: CR 702.87a: Level up is an activated ability that puts a level counter on this permanent. Activate only as a sorcery.

variants.name: Affinity
variants.doc: CR 702.41a: Affinity for [type] — this spell costs {1} less for each [type] you control.

variants.name: CumulativeUpkeep
variants.doc: CR 702.24a: Cumulative upkeep — triggered ability that imposes an increasing cost. The cost string contains the per-age-counter cost (mana, life payment, sacrifice, etc.).

variants.name: Hideaway
variants.doc: CR 702.74a: Hideaway N — look at top N cards, exile one face down, rest on bottom.

variants.name: Suspend
variants.doc: CR 702.62a: Suspend N—{cost} — exile from hand with N time counters, remove one each upkeep, cast without paying when last removed.

variants.name: Warp
variants.doc: Warp {cost}: alternative casting cost. Cast from hand for warp cost, exile at next end step, then may cast from exile later.

variants.name: Sneak
variants.doc: CR 702.190a: Sneak {cost} — alternative cost to cast this card from graveyard during the declare-blockers step by returning an unblocked attacker. CR 702.190b: the resulting permanent enters tapped and attacking the same defender.

variants.name: WebSlinging
variants.doc: CR 702.49 variant: Web-slinging — return a tapped creature to cast.

variants.name: Mobilize
variants.doc: Mobilize N — when this creature attacks, create N 1/1 red Warrior tokens tapped and attacking, sacrifice them at end of combat.

variants.name: Gift
variants.doc: Gift — optional casting-time promise. If promised, opponent receives the gift at resolution before the spell's other effects.

variants.name: Discover
variants.doc: CR 701.57a: Discover N — exile from top until nonland card with MV ≤ N.

variants.name: Toxic
variants.doc: CR 702.164: Toxic N — when this creature deals combat damage to a player, that player gets N poison counters.

variants.name: Saddle
variants.doc: CR 702.171a: Saddle N — tap creatures with total power N+ to saddle this Mount.

variants.name: Soulshift
variants.doc: CR 702.46: Soulshift N — when this creature dies, return target Spirit card with mana value N or less from your graveyard to your hand.

variants.name: Backup
variants.doc: CR 702.165: Backup N — when this creature enters, put N +1/+1 counters on target creature, which gains this creature's other abilities until EOT.

variants.name: Squad
variants.doc: CR 702.157: Squad {cost} — as an additional cost to cast, you may pay {cost} any number of times; ETB creates that many tokens.

variants.name: Typecycling
variants.doc: CR 702.29: Typecycling — "{subtype}cycling {cost}": discard this card and pay {cost} to search your library for a card with the specified subtype.

variants.name: Firebending
variants.doc: Firebending N — produces N {R} when this creature attacks (Avatar crossover).

variants.name: Splice
variants.doc: CR 702.46a: Splice onto [type] — reveal from hand and pay splice cost while casting a spell of the specified type to add this card's effects to that spell.

variants.name: Bargain
variants.doc: CR 702.166a: Bargain — you may sacrifice an artifact, enchantment, or token as an additional cost to cast this spell.

variants.name: Sunburst
variants.doc: CR 702.43a: Sunburst — enters with a counter for each color of mana spent to cast it.

variants.name: Champion
variants.doc: CR 702.72a: Champion a [type] — exile a creature of the specified type you control when this enters; return it when this leaves.

variants.name: Training
variants.doc: CR 702.149a: Training — whenever this creature attacks with another creature with greater power, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.

variants.name: Assist
variants.doc: CR 702.132a: Assist — another player can help pay the generic mana cost of this spell.

variants.name: JumpStart
variants.doc: CR 702.133a: Jump-start — cast from graveyard by discarding a card, then exile.

variants.name: Cipher
variants.doc: CR 702.98a: Cipher — exile this spell encoded on a creature you control; whenever that creature deals combat damage to a player, cast a copy.

variants.name: Transmute
variants.doc: CR 702.52a: Transmute {cost} — discard this card and pay {cost} to search your library for a card with the same mana value.

variants.name: Cleave
variants.doc: CR 702.148a: Cleave — alternative cost that removes bracketed text from Oracle text.

variants.name: Undaunted
variants.doc: CR 702.125a: Undaunted — costs {1} less for each opponent.

variants.name: Paradigm
variants.doc: CR 702.xxx: Paradigm (Strixhaven) — a keyword on instants/sorceries. Reminder: "Then exile this spell. After you first resolve a spell with this name, you may cast a copy of it from exile without paying its mana cost at the beginning of each of your first main phases." Runtime hooks in `stack.rs` (first-resolution arming) and `turns.rs` (first-main-phase offer) carry the semantics. Assign when WotC publishes SOS CR update.

variants.name: Station
variants.doc: CR 702.184a: Station — "Tap another untapped creature you control: Put a number of charge counters on this permanent equal to the tapped creature's power. Activate only as a sorcery." The keyword is fixed (no parameter) — the full semantics come from the rule text. Runtime activation is handled via `GameAction::ActivateStation`, not through the generic activated-ability dispatch.

variants.name: Unknown
variants.doc: Fallback for unrecognized keywords.

KeywordAction.doc: Typed payload for activated keyword abilities resolved from the stack.  CR 113.3b requires activated abilities to go on the stack. CR 113.7a requires last-known-information carry-through when the source leaves its zone. Cost-payment snapshots (e.g. `Station::snapshot_power`) are captured at announcement time so resolution is safe even if the paid creature leaves the battlefield.

variants.name: Equip
variants.doc: CR 702.6a: attach source equipment to target creature.

variants.name: Crew
variants.doc: CR 702.122a: the Vehicle becomes an artifact creature until end of turn. Paid-creature ids are captured for logging and trigger context only.

variants.name: Saddle
variants.doc: CR 702.171a: this permanent becomes saddled until end of turn. Paid-creature ids are captured for logging and trigger context only.

variants.name: Station
variants.doc: CR 702.184a: put charge counters on the Spacecraft equal to the tapped creature's power. The power value is snapshot at cost-payment time so resolution is stable under CR 113.7a even if the paid creature leaves the battlefield between announcement and resolution.

KeywordKind.doc: Discriminant-level keyword identity used when the Oracle text refers to a keyword class without caring about its parameter payload.

variants.name: Paradigm
variants.doc: CR 702.xxx: Paradigm (Strixhaven) — see `Keyword::Paradigm`.

variants.name: Miracle
variants.doc: CR 702.94: Miracle — see `Keyword::Miracle`.

Layer.doc: The seven layers of continuous effect evaluation per CR 613. Sublayers of layer 7 (P/T) are represented as separate variants.

variants.name: Copy
variants.doc: CR 613.1a: Layer 1 — Copy effects.

variants.name: Control
variants.doc: CR 613.1b: Layer 2 — Control-changing effects.

variants.name: Text
variants.doc: CR 613.1c: Layer 3 — Text-changing effects.

variants.name: Type
variants.doc: CR 613.1d: Layer 4 — Type-changing effects.

variants.name: Color
variants.doc: CR 613.1e: Layer 5 — Color-changing effects.

variants.name: Ability
variants.doc: CR 613.1f: Layer 6 — Ability-adding and ability-removing effects.

variants.name: CharDef
variants.doc: CR 613.4a: Layer 7a — Characteristic-defining abilities that set P/T.

variants.name: SetPT
variants.doc: CR 613.4b: Layer 7b — Effects that set P/T to specific values.

variants.name: ModifyPT
variants.doc: CR 613.4c: Layer 7c — Effects that modify P/T (+N/+N).

variants.name: SwitchPT
variants.doc: CR 613.4d: Layer 7d — Effects that switch P/T.

variants.name: CounterPT
variants.doc: CR 613.4c: Layer 7e — +1/+1 and -1/-1 counters modifying P/T (applied after other 7c effects).

LayoutKind.doc: Runtime layout discriminant for double-faced cards.  Stored on `BackFaceData` so the engine can distinguish Modal DFCs (which allow face-choice per CR 712.12) from Transform DFCs at runtime. Intentionally separate from `database::synthesis::LayoutKind` which is a build-pipeline type without serialization derives.

variants.name: Prepare
variants.doc: CR 702.xxx: Prepare (Strixhaven) frame mechanic — a two-face card whose face `b` is a "prepare spell" (Sorcery/Instant). When face `a` is prepared, a copy of face `b` can be cast. Structurally an Adventure analog. Assign when WotC publishes SOS CR update.

LearnOption.doc: CR 701.48a: Learn choice — rummage a specific card, or skip entirely.

variants.name: Rummage
variants.doc: Discard the specified card, then draw one.

variants.name: Skip
variants.doc: Decline to learn (skip).

LibraryPosition.doc: CR 701.24g: Specific position within a library for placement effects. Top and Bottom use move_to_library_position; NthFromTop inserts at index n-1.

variants.name: NthFromTop
variants.doc: "second from the top", "third from the top", "seventh from the top"

LifeAmount.doc: How much life is gained — a fixed amount or derived from the targeted permanent.

variants.name: Fixed
variants.doc: Gain a specific number of life.

variants.name: TargetPower
variants.doc: Gain life equal to the targeted permanent's power.

LinkedExileScope.doc: CR 607.2a + CR 406.6 + CR 610.3: Which exile-link relation a mana ability reads when computing legal colors. Typed enum — never a bool — so future extensions (e.g. links to a different host than `~` itself) slot in cleanly.

variants.name: ThisObject
variants.doc: Read `state.exile_links` keyed to this ability's source object (the activating permanent).

LogCategory.doc: Categories for log entries, used for verbosity filtering in the frontend.

LogSegment.doc: A typed segment of a log entry, enabling rich UI rendering.

ManaChoice.doc: CR 605.3b: Player's answer to a `ManaChoicePrompt`, carried by `GameAction::ChooseManaColor`. Shape mirrors the prompt variant.

ManaChoicePrompt.doc: CR 605.3b: The shape of the prompt surfaced via `WaitingFor::ChooseManaColor`. Typed enum rather than a bool discriminator: the continuation logic is identical (validate choice → produce mana → resume), only the option set differs.

variants.name: SingleColor
variants.doc: Legacy prompt shape: pick one color from the list (Treasure, City of Brass, Pit of Offerings, `AnyOneColor`).

variants.name: Combination
variants.doc: Filter-land prompt: pick one complete multi-mana combination.

ManaContribution.doc: CR 605.1a + CR 107.4a: Whether a mana-production effect is the *base* mana addition (e.g. a basic Forest tapping for `{G}`) or an *additional* mana addition that piggy-backs on another mana event (e.g. Fertile Ground's "adds an additional one mana"). Typed enum — never a bool — so the parser and resolver can dispatch on the contribution role without conflating it with other dimensions of mana production.

variants.name: Base
variants.doc: The mana addition stands on its own (most mana abilities).

variants.name: Additional
variants.doc: The mana addition is additive — it augments another mana event (Utopia Sprawl, Fertile Ground, Wild Growth, Carpet of Flowers).

variants.name: SelfManaCost
variants.doc: The card's own mana cost (used for "the flashback cost is equal to its mana cost").

ManaExpiry.doc: When mana expires — controls lifecycle beyond the normal CR 500.4 phase drain.

variants.name: EndOfCombat
variants.doc: Mana persists through combat steps but drains at EndCombat → PostCombatMain. Used by Firebending and similar "mana lasts within combat" mechanics.

ManaModification.doc: CR 106.3 + CR 614.1a: Mana-production replacement payload. Used by `ReplacementEvent::ProduceMana` to describe HOW the produced mana should be modified before entering the player's mana pool.

variants.name: ReplaceWith
variants.doc: CR 614.1a: Replace with a specific mana type regardless of what was produced. e.g., Contamination ("produces {B} instead"), Pale Moon ("produces colorless instead").

ManaPip.doc: Display-layer projection of `ManaProduction` — typed pip descriptors the frontend renders verbatim. One variant per `ManaProduction` axis so no information is lost on the wire (e.g., colorless producers must surface as a `Colorless` pip rather than an empty `Vec<ManaColor>`).  The frontend treats this as opaque display data; all derivation lives in the engine. Per the "build for the class" rule, every `ManaProduction` variant maps to a `ManaPip` here so future variants force an exhaustive `match` update.

variants.name: Color
variants.doc: CR 106.1a: A specific color of mana ({W}, {U}, {B}, {R}, {G}).

variants.name: Colorless
variants.doc: CR 106.1b: Colorless mana ({C}).

variants.name: OneOfColors
variants.doc: CR 106.4: Producer chooses one color from the listed set, then yields N of that color. When the set is all five colors this represents "any color" (City of Brass); the frontend may special-case the 5-of-5 case visually.

variants.name: CombinationOfColors
variants.doc: CR 106.4: Producer assigns each unit independently across the listed color set (e.g., Cascading Cataracts). Same axis as `OneOfColors` but per-unit choice.

variants.name: AnyInCommandersIdentity
variants.doc: CR 903.4: Producer adds one mana of any color in the controller's commander color identity (Command Tower, Path of Ancestry). The frontend resolves the pip set against the controller's `commander_color_identity`.

ManaProduction.doc: Mana production descriptor for `Effect::Mana`.  Custom Deserialize: accepts both the tagged format `{"type":"Fixed","colors":["White"]}` (new) and a plain array of `ManaColor` like `["White","Green"]` (legacy, pre-ManaProduction refactor).

variants.name: Fixed
variants.doc: Produce an explicit fixed sequence of colored mana symbols (e.g. `{W}{U}`).

variants.name: Colorless
variants.doc: Produce N colorless mana (e.g. `{C}`, `{C}{C}`).

variants.name: Mixed
variants.doc: CR 106.1: Produce a mix of colorless and colored mana (e.g. `{C}{W}`, `{C}{C}{R}`). Used by Ravnica bounce lands (Karoo, Azorius Chancery) and similar.

variants.name: AnyOneColor
variants.doc: Produce N mana of one chosen color from the provided set.

variants.name: AnyCombination
variants.doc: Produce N mana where each unit can be chosen independently from the provided set.

variants.name: ChosenColor
variants.doc: Produce N mana of a previously chosen color.

variants.name: OpponentLandColors
variants.doc: CR 106.7: Produce mana of any color that a land an opponent controls could produce. Colors are computed dynamically at resolution time by inspecting opponent lands.

variants.name: AnyTypeProduceableBy
variants.doc: CR 106.7 + CR 106.1b: Produce N mana of any **type** (W/U/B/R/G/C) that a land matching `land_filter` could produce. Differs from `OpponentLandColors` in that the choice axis is the full type set including colorless (Reflecting Pool, Naga Vitalist, Incubation Druid, Cactus Preserve, Horizon of Progress). The `land_filter` controls which lands contribute (typically `ControllerRef::You`, but parameterized so future opponent-/player-scoped printings slot in without a new variant). Per CR 106.7 the union ignores cost-payability of the surveyed lands' mana abilities — only the resulting *type set* matters. CR 106.5 applies when the union is empty (e.g. no matching lands → no mana).

variants.name: ChoiceAmongExiledColors
variants.doc: CR 605.1a + CR 406.1: Produce one mana of any of the colors among the cards linked to `source` via `state.exile_links` (e.g. Pit of Offerings — "Add one mana of any of the exiled cards' colors"). Colors are computed dynamically at resolution time; if no linked card is colored the ability produces no mana (CR 106.5).

variants.name: ChoiceAmongCombinations
variants.doc: CR 605.3b + CR 106.1a: Produce one of several fixed multi-mana combinations chosen by the controller. Each option is a complete pre-specified sequence of mana types (e.g. Shadowmoor/Eventide filter lands: `Add {W}{W}, {W}{U}, or {U}{U}` yields options `[[W,W], [W,U], [U,U]]`). Unlike `AnyOneColor` (pick one color, repeat N times) the choice axis here is which complete combination to produce.

variants.name: AnyInCommandersColorIdentity
variants.doc: CR 903.4 + CR 903.4f + CR 106.5: Produce N mana of one color chosen from the controller's commander(s)' combined color identity. Colors are computed dynamically at resolution time via `commander_color_identity`. If the color identity is empty (no commander, or a colorless commander), CR 106.5 applies and the ability produces no mana. Used by Path of Ancestry and Study Hall.

variants.name: DistinctColorsAmongPermanents
variants.doc: CR 106.1 + CR 109.1: Produce one mana of each distinct color among permanents matching a filter. "Gold", "multicolor", and "colorless" are not colors (CR 105.1), so each of W/U/B/R/G contributes at most once. Used by Faeburrow Elder's "{T}: For each color among permanents you control, add one mana of that color." Mirrors the structure of `QuantityRef::DistinctColorsAmongPermanents`.

variants.name: TriggerEventManaType
variants.doc: CR 603.7c + CR 106.3: Produce one mana of the same type as the mana produced by the triggering `ManaAdded` event. Used by `TapsForMana` triggers of the form "add one mana of any type that land produced" (Vorinclex, Voice of Hunger; Dictate of Karametra). Resolves from `state.current_trigger_event` at resolution time; emits no mana if the current trigger event is absent or not a `ManaAdded` event (CR 106.5).

variants.name: OnlyForSpellType
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to cast creature spells" / "only to cast artifact spells".

variants.name: OnlyForCreatureType
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to cast a creature spell of the chosen type." The `String` is the chosen creature type (e.g., "Elf").

variants.name: OnlyForTypeSpellsOrAbilities
variants.doc: CR 106.6: "Spend this mana only to cast creature spells or activate abilities of creatures." Allows spending for spells of the type (checked via `allows_spell`) OR for ability activations on permanents of the type (checked via `allows_activation`).

variants.name: OnlyForActivation
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to activate abilities." Cannot be used for casting spells — activation-only.

variants.name: OnlyForXCosts
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only on costs that include {X}." Only permits spending on spells or abilities with {X} in their cost.

variants.name: OnlyForSpellWithKeywordKind
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to cast spells with flashback."

variants.name: OnlyForSpellWithKeywordKindFromZone
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to cast spells with flashback from a graveyard."

ManaSpellGrant.doc: CR 106.6: Additional effect that the mana confers upon the spell it is spent on. E.g., "that spell can't be countered" (Cavern of Souls, Delighted Halfling).

variants.name: CantBeCountered
variants.doc: The spell cast with this mana can't be countered.

ManaSpendRestriction.doc: Parse-time template for mana spend restrictions.  Unlike [`ManaRestriction`](super::mana::ManaRestriction) which carries concrete values on a `ManaUnit`, this enum is stored on `Effect::Mana` and resolved at production time by reading runtime state (e.g., chosen creature type from the source object).

variants.name: SpellType
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to cast creature spells."

variants.name: ChosenCreatureType
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to cast a creature spell of the chosen type." Resolved at runtime from the source's `chosen_creature_type()`.

variants.name: SpellTypeOrAbilityActivation
variants.doc: CR 106.12: "Spend this mana only to cast creature spells or activate abilities of creatures." Combined restriction with OR semantics: allowed for spells of the type OR ability activations on permanents of the type. The `String` is the card type (e.g., "Creature").

variants.name: ActivateOnly
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to activate abilities." Cannot be used to cast spells; only for ability activation costs.

variants.name: XCostOnly
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only on costs that include {X}." Only permits spending on spells or abilities with {X} in their cost.

variants.name: SpellWithKeywordKind
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to cast spells with flashback."

variants.name: SpellWithKeywordKindFromZone
variants.doc: "Spend this mana only to cast spells with flashback from a graveyard."

ModalSelectionConstraint.doc: Selection constraints attached to a modal choice header.

variants.name: NoRepeatThisTurn
variants.doc: CR 700.2: Each mode may only be chosen once per turn for this source. Oracle text: "choose one that hasn't been chosen this turn"

variants.name: NoRepeatThisGame
variants.doc: CR 700.2: Each mode may only be chosen once total for this source. Oracle text: "choose one that hasn't been chosen"

NextSpellModifier.doc: CR 601.2f: The kind of modification to apply to the next qualifying spell.

variants.name: CantBeCountered
variants.doc: "The next spell you cast this turn can't be countered."

variants.name: HasKeyword
variants.doc: "The next spell you cast this turn has [keyword]."

variants.name: CastAsThoughFlash
variants.doc: "The next spell you cast this turn can be cast as though it had flash."

NinjutsuVariant.doc: CR 702.49: Ninjutsu-family keyword variants that share the "activate to swap a creature in combat" pattern. This enum is the *activation-family dispatch* layer — it is used only by `activate_ninjutsu` and its supporting helpers (`ninjutsu_timing_ok`, `returnable_creatures_for_variant`, etc.) to pick the correct activation behavior. Sneak (CR 702.190a) is NOT an activated ability and therefore does not appear here; see `CastVariantPaid` for the trigger-tag layer that does include it.

variants.name: Ninjutsu
variants.doc: CR 702.49a: Return unblocked attacker, declare blockers or later.

variants.name: CommanderNinjutsu
variants.doc: CR 702.49d: Commander ninjutsu — activate from hand or command zone.

variants.name: WebSlinging
variants.doc: CR 702.49 variant: Return any tapped creature you control.

ObjectProperty.doc: A measurable property of a game object for aggregate queries.

OpponentMayScope.doc: CR 608.2d: Who may choose to perform an optional effect during resolution. Used with `AbilityDefinition::optional_for` to route the "you may" prompt to opponents.

variants.name: AnyOpponent
variants.doc: "any opponent may" — each opponent in APNAP order gets the chance; first accept wins.

Parity.doc: Odd or even — used by cards like "choose odd or even."

ParsedCondition.doc: CR 601.3 / CR 602.5: A fully typed condition for casting/activation restrictions. Parsed at Oracle parse time to eliminate runtime reparsing. `Option<ParsedCondition>` is used at storage sites — `None` means the parser could not decompose the condition (permissive fallback: evaluates to `true`).

variants.name: SourceIsAttacking
variants.doc: CR 508.1a: The source creature is currently attacking.

variants.name: SourceIsBlocked
variants.doc: CR 509.1h: The source creature is blocked.

variants.name: SourceEnteredThisTurn
variants.doc: CR 302.6: Source entered the battlefield this turn.

variants.name: QuantityVsEachOpponent
variants.doc: Compares a player-relative quantity against each opponent's quantity. The comparison must hold for ALL opponents.

variants.name: CreatureDiedThisTurn
variants.doc: CR 700.4: A creature moved from battlefield to graveyard this turn.

variants.name: PlayerCountAtLeast
variants.doc: CR 602.5b: Count of non-eliminated players matching `filter` is at least `minimum`. e.g. "an opponent lost life this turn" → `filter: OpponentLostLife, minimum: 1`

PartnerType.doc: CR 702.124: Partner variant keywords for co-commander deckbuilding. Each variant specifies which other partner types it can legally pair with.

variants.name: Generic
variants.doc: CR 702.124a: Generic "Partner" — pairs with any other generic Partner.

variants.name: With
variants.doc: CR 702.124c: "Partner with [Name]" — pairs only with the named card.

variants.name: FriendsForever
variants.doc: CR 702.124f: "Friends forever" — pairs with any other Friends Forever card.

variants.name: CharacterSelect
variants.doc: CR 702.124: "Partner—Character select" — pairs with any other Character Select card.

variants.name: DoctorsCompanion
variants.doc: CR 702.124: "Doctor's companion" — pairs with any creature with the Doctor subtype.

variants.name: ChooseABackground
variants.doc: CR 702.124: "Choose a Background" — pairs with any enchantment with Background subtype.

PayableResource.doc: CR 107.14 + CR 118.8: Resource that can be paid in a "pay any amount of X" prompt. Typed so the same `WaitingFor::PayAmountChoice` variant generalizes to future classes (energy, life, mana) without re-introducing boolean flags.

variants.name: Energy
variants.doc: CR 107.14: Pay any amount of `{E}` — removes N energy counters from the player.

PaymentContext.doc: CR 106.6: Context for a mana-payment decision. Distinguishes "paying for a spell being cast" from "paying for an ability being activated" so the restriction check can route through `allows_spell` vs `allows_activation`.  Casting-restricted mana (e.g., "creature-spell-only") must reject ability activations; activation-restricted mana (e.g., "activate abilities only") must reject spell casts. Using the correct variant per payment site is the single authority that enforces this bifurcation.

variants.name: Spell
variants.doc: Payment for a spell being cast — consult `allows_spell`.

variants.name: Activation
variants.doc: Payment for an activated ability — consult `allows_activation` using the source permanent's core types and subtypes.

PaymentCost.doc: CR 118.1: A cost paid as part of an effect's resolution. Distinct from AbilityCost (which gates activation before the colon).

variants.name: Life
variants.doc: CR 118.8 + CR 119.4: Pay life during effect resolution. Paying life IS losing life — the replacement pipeline and `CantLoseLife` locks apply. `amount` is a `QuantityExpr` so dynamic references (`"life equal to its power"`, `"X life"`, `"life equal to the number of ..."`) compose through the same cost resolver as a literal `"pay 2 life"`.

variants.name: Speed
variants.doc: CR 118.3 + CR 702.179f: Pay speed during effect resolution.

variants.name: Energy
variants.doc: CR 107.14: Pay energy counters during effect resolution. Distinct from `AbilityCost::PayEnergy` (activation cost before the colon).

PermissionGrantee.doc: CR 611.2a + CR 108.3: Identifies which player a `CastingPermission` is granted to at resolution time. Resolved to a concrete `PlayerId` by `grant_permission::resolve` before the permission is written to the target `GameObject`. Drives both `granted_to` binding and, for durations scoped to that player (e.g., `UntilNextTurnOf`), the prune step in `layers.rs`.  Default is `AbilityController` for all pre-existing parser call sites. Additional variants support compound-exile patterns where multiple objects are granted distinct permissions tied to different players: - `ObjectOwner` — Suspend Aggression: "its owner may play it". Each object   in the tracked set is granted to its own owner (CR 108.3). - `ParentTargetController` — Expedited Inheritance: "its controller may   exile [N] ... They may play those cards". The grant is tied to the   parent effect's player target rather than the triggered-ability controller.

variants.name: AbilityController
variants.doc: CR 611.2a default — the controller of the effect that created the grant.

variants.name: ObjectOwner
variants.doc: CR 108.3 — each iterated object's owner (per-object binding).

variants.name: ParentTargetController
variants.doc: CR 109.4 — the player target of the parent effect in the chain.

Phase.doc: Represents the phases and steps of a turn (CR 500.1).  A turn consists of five phases: beginning, precombat main, combat, postcombat main, and ending. The beginning, combat, and ending phases are further broken down into steps.

variants.name: Untap
variants.doc: CR 502: Untap step. No player receives priority (CR 502.4).

variants.name: Upkeep
variants.doc: CR 503: Upkeep step. Active player gets priority after triggered abilities are put on stack.

variants.name: Draw
variants.doc: CR 504: Draw step. Active player draws a card as a turn-based action (CR 504.1).

variants.name: PreCombatMain
variants.doc: CR 505.1: Precombat main phase. Players may cast non-instant spells (CR 505.6a).

variants.name: BeginCombat
variants.doc: CR 507: Beginning of combat step.

variants.name: DeclareAttackers
variants.doc: CR 508: Declare attackers step. Active player declares attackers (CR 508.1).

variants.name: DeclareBlockers
variants.doc: CR 509: Declare blockers step. Defending player declares blockers (CR 509.1).

variants.name: CombatDamage
variants.doc: CR 510: Combat damage step. Attacking/blocking creatures assign and deal damage.

variants.name: EndCombat
variants.doc: CR 511: End of combat step. "At end of combat" triggered abilities trigger.

variants.name: PostCombatMain
variants.doc: CR 505.1: Postcombat main phase. Follows the combat phase.

variants.name: End
variants.doc: CR 513: End step. "At the beginning of the end step" abilities trigger.

variants.name: Cleanup
variants.doc: CR 514: Cleanup step. Active player discards to hand size, damage is removed (CR 514.1).

PlayerCounterKind.doc: CR 122.1b: Named player counter types tracked by the engine. Poison counters route to the dedicated `poison_counters` field due to SBA rules (CR 704.5c). Energy counters are excluded — they use the dedicated `energy` field and `GainEnergy` effect.

PlayerFilter.doc: A filter matching players by game-state conditions.

variants.name: Controller
variants.doc: The controller of the effect or quantity.

variants.name: Opponent
variants.doc: All opponents of the controller.

variants.name: OpponentLostLife
variants.doc: Each opponent who lost life this turn (life_lost_this_turn > 0).

variants.name: OpponentGainedLife
variants.doc: Each opponent who gained life this turn (life_gained_this_turn > 0).

variants.name: All
variants.doc: All players.

variants.name: HighestSpeed
variants.doc: CR 702.179f: Each player whose speed is tied for the highest speed among players.

variants.name: ZoneChangedThisWay
variants.doc: "each player who [verb]ed a card this way" — scoped to players who owned objects that changed zones in the preceding effect (tracked via `last_zone_changed_ids`).

variants.name: OwnersOfCardsExiledBySource
variants.doc: Each owner of a card currently exiled with the ability's source. Used by linked-exile follow-ups like Skyclave Apparition's leaves trigger.

variants.name: TriggeringPlayer
variants.doc: CR 113.3c + CR 603.2: The player identified by `state.current_trigger_event`. Used to route "they [verb]" effects on triggers whose subject is a player (e.g. Firemane Commando's "another player ... they draw a card").

variants.name: OpponentOtherThanTriggering
variants.doc: CR 120.3 + CR 603.2c: Each opponent other than the player identified by `state.current_trigger_event`. Used by "each other opponent" phrasing on damage triggers — the triggering opponent has already received the source's damage in the same chain (e.g. Hydra Omnivore's "Whenever ~ deals combat damage to an opponent, it deals that much damage to each other opponent."). The "other" anaphors back to the triggering opponent named in the trigger event clause. Falls back to plain `Opponent` semantics when no trigger event is in scope (i.e. only excludes the controller).

PlayerScope.doc: CR 102 + CR 119 + CR 402: Player axis for player-scoped quantity references.  Parameterizes the player whose hand size, life total, or other per-player scalar is being read. Replaces sibling enum variants like `LifeTotal` / `TargetLifeTotal` / `OpponentLifeTotal` and `HandSize` / `OpponentHandSize` with a single parameterized form.  Categorical-boundary check (per the "Parameterize, don't proliferate" principle): every variant is a player-relativity choice within a single CR section. Aggregate scopes (`Opponent`, `AllPlayers`) compose `AggregateFunction` to express max/min/sum across a population — they do not introduce a new abstraction layer.

variants.name: Controller
variants.doc: CR 109.5 / CR 113.6: The controller of the source ability or effect.

variants.name: Target
variants.doc: CR 109.4 + CR 113.6 + CR 115.1: The first player target of the resolving ability (read from `ability.targets`).

variants.name: Opponent
variants.doc: CR 102.2 + CR 102.3: All opponents of the controller, aggregated by `aggregate`. Existing `OpponentLifeTotal` / `OpponentHandSize` semantics correspond to `aggregate = Max`.

variants.name: AllPlayers
variants.doc: CR 102.1: All players in the game (controller + opponents), aggregated by `aggregate`. Reserved for future cards that read "the highest life total among players" or similar cross-player extrema that include the controller.

PlayerStatus.doc: Status of a player in the game. Mirrors `PhaseStatus` for permanents — a phased-out player is treated as though they don't exist for targeting, damage, attacking, and SBA loss-from-life purposes, but they remain in the game state (never removed from `state.players`). Their phased-out turn proceeds with the player still as the active player; the status is cleared at the next time a `Duration::UntilNextTurnOf` effect that phased them out would expire (the active player's untap step).  CR 702.26 governs *permanent* phasing only; the player-phasing semantics are derived from card Oracle text on the small set of cards (e.g., historical Teferi's Protection wording) that say "you phase out". The invariants below mirror the permanent-phasing invariants:  - Status is the sole encoding of phased-in vs phased-out — the player   never leaves `state.players`. - While phased out, the player can't be targeted, attacked, dealt damage,   or lose the game from 0-or-less life. These exclusions live at single   choke points (`game/targeting.rs::add_players`,   `game/combat.rs::get_valid_attack_targets`,   `game/effects/deal_damage.rs::apply_damage_after_replacement`,   `game/sba.rs::check_player_life`).

PreventionAmount.doc: CR 615: How much damage to prevent.

variants.name: Next
variants.doc: "Prevent the next N damage"

variants.name: All
variants.doc: "Prevent all damage"

variants.name: AllDamage
variants.doc: Prevent all damage (combat + noncombat).

variants.name: CombatDamage
variants.doc: Prevent only combat damage.

ProductionOverride.doc: CR 605.3b + CR 106.1a: A pre-resolved choice that short-circuits the normal `ChooseManaColor` prompt. Auto-tap sets this when the cost-payment planner has already determined the exact mana to produce; manual activation leaves it `None` so the player is prompted.  Typed enum (never a bool): `SingleColor` covers the one-color-repeated variants (`AnyOneColor`, `ChoiceAmongExiledColors`), while `Combination` carries the full pre-chosen multi-mana sequence for `ChoiceAmongCombinations` (Shadowmoor/Eventide filter lands).

variants.name: SingleColor
variants.doc: The caller picked a single color; every unit of mana the ability produces becomes this color (mirrors the pre-widening `Option<ManaType>` semantics).

variants.name: Combination
variants.doc: The caller picked one complete combination from a `ChoiceAmongCombinations` option set; the ability produces exactly these mana types in order.

ProhibitionScope.doc: CR 109.5 + CR 102.1: The "who" axis of a continuous prohibition static.  Shared across the prohibition family (casting, drawing, searching, activating). CR 109.5: The words "you" and "your" on an object refer to the object's controller. CR 102.1: "opponent" is defined relative to a given player's controller. Wire format (`Display` / `FromStr`) is preserved: `"opponents"`, `"all_players"`, `"controller"`, `"enchanted_creature_controller"` — do NOT change these strings, they are serialized into card-data JSON.

variants.name: Opponents
variants.doc: "your opponents" — only the controller's opponents are prohibited.

variants.name: AllPlayers
variants.doc: "players" / "each player" — all players are prohibited.

variants.name: Controller
variants.doc: "you" — only the controller is prohibited.

variants.name: EnchantedCreatureController
variants.doc: "enchanted creature's controller" — the controller of the creature this aura enchants. CR 303.4e: Used by auras that restrict the enchanted creature's controller.

variants.name: CreateToken
variants.doc: CR 111.1 + CR 614.1a: Token creation event carrying the full self-describing token specification. Replacement effects can modify `count` (Doubling Season, Primal Vigor) or inspect `spec` for characteristic-based gating (e.g., "whenever a creature token you control would enter ...").  `spec` is boxed so this variant doesn't dominate the enum size — `TokenSpec` is ~400 bytes of resolved characteristics, and most other variants are small IDs.

variants.name: BeginTurn
variants.doc: CR 500.1 + CR 614.1b + CR 614.10: A turn is about to begin. Carried through the replacement pipeline so condition-gated skip effects (e.g., Stranglehold's "skip extra turns") can prevent the turn.  `is_extra_turn` is true when this turn was granted by an effect (CR 500.7 — popped from `state.extra_turns`).

variants.name: BeginPhase
variants.doc: CR 500.1 + CR 614.1b: A phase/step is about to begin. Carried through the replacement pipeline so condition-gated skip effects can prevent the phase. Simple static-based skips (`StaticMode::SkipStep`) continue to short-circuit earlier in `turns.rs`; this pipeline path handles event-context-aware replacements.

variants.name: ProduceMana
variants.doc: CR 106.3 + CR 614.1a: Mana is about to be produced by a source and added to a player's mana pool. Carried through the replacement pipeline so static effects like Contamination ("produces {B} instead") can replace the produced mana type before it enters the pool.

ProtectionTarget.doc: What a Protection keyword protects from (CR 702.16).

variants.name: ChosenColor
variants.doc: CR 702.16: Protection from the chosen color — resolved at runtime from the source permanent's `chosen_attributes`.

variants.name: Everything
variants.doc: CR 702.16j: "Protection from everything" — protection from each object regardless of that object's characteristic values. Matches every source in `source_matches_protection_target`.

PtValue.doc: Power/toughness value -- either a fixed integer or a variable reference (e.g. "*", "X").  Custom Deserialize: accepts both the tagged format `{"type":"Fixed","value":2}` (new) and plain strings like `"2"` or `"*"` (legacy card-data.json).

QuantityExpr.doc: An expression that produces an integer for quantity comparisons. Either a dynamic game-state lookup or a literal constant.

variants.name: Ref
variants.doc: A dynamic quantity looked up from the current game state.

variants.name: Fixed
variants.doc: A literal integer constant.

variants.name: HalfRounded
variants.doc: CR 107.1a: "Half X, rounded up/down" — divides the inner expression by 2 in the rounding direction specified by the Oracle text.

variants.name: Offset
variants.doc: CR 604.3: Base expression plus a fixed integer offset. "N plus the number of X" / "that number plus N" patterns.

variants.name: Multiply
variants.doc: "Twice the number of X" / "N times X" / negation via factor: -1.

variants.name: Sum
variants.doc: Sum of N independent quantity expressions. Used for conjunctive "for each X and each Y" patterns where the two filters span disjoint zones or axes that cannot be expressed as a single `TargetFilter::Or` (e.g., Alrund's "+1/+1 for each card in your hand and each foretold card you own in exile").

variants.name: UpTo
variants.doc: CR 107.1c + CR 608.2d: "Up to N" — the affected player chooses any integer in `0..=resolve(max)` at resolution time. Used by `Effect::Draw`, `Effect::Sacrifice`, `Effect::Discard`, and `Effect::SearchLibrary` for the "draw / sacrifice / discard / search for up to N" Oracle text class. The 4 specific resolvers peel this wrapper via `QuantityExpr::peel_up_to` and propagate the bool to their `WaitingFor::*Choice` runtime state.  Layered above `QuantityExpr::Ref`/`Fixed`/`HalfRounded`/etc. so the upper bound itself can be a dynamic game-state quantity (e.g. "draw up to your hand size cards" → `UpTo { max: Ref { qty: HandSize } }`, "sacrifice up to half your creatures" → `UpTo { max: HalfRounded { inner: Ref { qty: ObjectCount {..} }, rounding: Down } }`).  Generic quantity resolvers (`resolve_quantity`, `resolve_quantity_with_targets`, etc.) treat `UpTo` transparently — they resolve to the upper bound as if the `UpTo` wrapper were not present. This is safe because the only places where the "may pick fewer" semantics matter are the 4 specific effect resolvers, which extract the flag explicitly via `peel_up_to`.  Invariant: `max` MUST NOT itself be `UpTo` — nesting is meaningless ("up to up to N" is just "up to N"). Always construct via the `QuantityExpr::up_to` helper which debug-asserts this invariant.

QuantityModification.doc: CR 614.1a: Quantity modification for replacement effects (tokens, counters). Modeled after DamageModification but for non-damage quantities.

variants.name: Double
variants.doc: count * 2 — Primal Vigor, Doubling Season, Parallel Lives, Anointed Procession

variants.name: Plus
variants.doc: count + value — Hardened Scales (+1)

variants.name: Minus
variants.doc: count.saturating_sub(value) — Vizier of Remedies (-1)

QuantityRef.doc: A dynamic game quantity — a runtime lookup into the game state.

variants.name: HandSize
variants.doc: CR 402: Number of cards in `player`'s hand. `PlayerScope::Controller` is the default reading; `Target`, `Opponent { .. }`, and `AllPlayers` cover targeted-player and cross-player aggregate variants.

variants.name: LifeTotal
variants.doc: CR 119: `player`'s current life total. See `HandSize` for player-axis semantics.

variants.name: GraveyardSize
variants.doc: Number of cards in the controller's graveyard.

variants.name: LifeAboveStarting
variants.doc: Controller's life total minus the format's starting life total. Used for "N or more life more than your starting life total" conditions.

variants.name: StartingLifeTotal
variants.doc: CR 103.4: The format's starting life total (20 for Standard, 40 for Commander, etc.).

variants.name: ObjectCount
variants.doc: Count of objects on the battlefield matching a filter. Used for "for each creature you control" and similar patterns.

variants.name: ObjectCountDistinctNames
variants.doc: CR 201.2 + CR 603.4: Count of distinct names among objects matching a filter. Used for "seven or more lands with different names" (Field of the Dead) and similar distinct-name threshold predicates. Two objects with the same name count once.

variants.name: PlayerCount
variants.doc: Count of players matching a player-level filter. Used for "for each opponent who lost life this turn" and similar patterns.

variants.name: CountersOnSelf
